strategy: Round 1 he Polymorphs every wizard into a random Vegetable form (Life, Storm, Death, or Ice) for 15 rounds - you fight with that form's deck, not your own spells. Plan to win inside the polymorph.
The Death Vegetable form is best if you get it; he takes 30% more from Death and resists Life 54%.
Do not Feint him - Feint or Mass Feint makes him Cleanse Ward and strip all his negative wards. Use the polymorph's own blades and hit.
He re-polymorphs a random wizard on a timer set by party size (every 3rd round with 4 wizards, up to every 9th solo), so expect forms to shift; keep hitting.
Do not join late - a latecomer eats a Grim Grrnadier.
Watch his spell tells: Lamassu on the lowest-health wizard, Grim Grrnadier on the highest, Wings of Night, Hungry Caterpillar - spread health and heal accordingly.
cheatNotes: A wizard who joins after round 1 is hit with Grim Grrnadier. Enter on time.
Feint or Mass Feint makes him Cleanse Ward off all his negative wards. Don't rely on traps; hit with blades instead.
Round 1 he Polymorphs every wizard into a Vegetable form for 15 rounds - you use that form's deck, not your own.
Possible form: Death Vegetable - best against him (he takes +30% Death).
He also re-polymorphs a random wizard into a random Vegetable on a timer set by party size.
Solo, the re-polymorph hits every 9th round.
"Bringing down the Veghammer": he casts Lamassu on the lowest-health wizard.
"As the World Turnips": he casts Wings of Night.
"Green Beanzooka": he casts Grim Grrnadier on the highest-health wizard.
"I find you in contempt of corn": he casts Hungry Caterpillar on a random wizard.
